machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ABC ATPase; energises export and forms the IM channel
machinery_genes.quote: five open reading frames: aprA, the structural gene encoding APR; aprI, which encodes a protease inhibitor; and aprD, aprE, aprF whose products are involved in protease secretion. The AprD, AprE and AprF proteins share significant homology with proteins implicated in secretion of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
machinery_genes.note: Apparatus named in P. aeruginosa (Duong et al. 1992), the archetypal Apr alkaline-protease T1SS. Transferred to P. entomophila L48, where the apr protease cluster (effector PSEEN1550 = AprA homolog) carries the same AprDEF exporter. AprD is the ABC transporter component (brief vocabulary: HlyB/PrtD/AprD family).

machinery_genes.role: periplasm-spanning, IM-anchored adaptor bridging the ABC transporter to the OMP
machinery_genes.quote: five open reading frames: aprA, the structural gene encoding APR; aprI, which encodes a protease inhibitor; and aprD, aprE, aprF whose products are involved in protease secretion. The AprD, AprE and AprF proteins share significant homology with proteins implicated in secretion of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
machinery_genes.note: MFP / HlyD-family adaptor of the Apr T1SS (brief vocabulary: HlyD/PrtE/AprE). NCBI annotates PA1247/NP_249938.1 as 'alkaline protease secretion protein AprE'. Strain transfer from P. aeruginosa as above.

machinery_genes.role: TolC-family outer-membrane channel forming the exit duct
machinery_genes.quote: five open reading frames: aprA, the structural gene encoding APR; aprI, which encodes a protease inhibitor; and aprD, aprE, aprF whose products are involved in protease secretion. The AprD, AprE and AprF proteins share significant homology with proteins implicated in secretion of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
machinery_genes.note: OMP / TolC-family channel of the Apr T1SS (brief vocabulary: TolC/PrtF/AprF). NCBI annotates PA0427-equivalent NP_249939.1 as 'alkaline protease secretion protein AprF'. Strain transfer from P. aeruginosa as above. Disruption of aprF abolishes alkaline-protease secretion (S2950194625002316).

notes: System = the Apr alkaline-protease Type I secretion system. The effector of this instance, PSEEN1550, is the P. entomophila L48 homolog of AprA, a serralysin-family zinc metalloprotease secreted one-step across both membranes by a three-component ABC-exporter (AprD ABC transporter + AprE membrane fusion protein + AprF TolC-family OMP), with the substrate carrying a C-terminal (non-cleaved) secretion signal. The instance machinery label PSEEN1552 falls within the apr secretion cluster adjacent to aprA. APPARATUS PAPER STRAIN TRANSFER: the individual genes aprD/aprE/aprF are named and characterised in Pseudomonas aeruginosa by Duong, Lazdunski, Cami & Murgier (1992, Gene), the founding sequencing paper of the apr cluster; Duong et al. 2001 confirm aprD/-E/-F form a single operon ('organized in one single operon together with the aprD, -E and -F genes'). The P. entomophila L48 genome paper (Vodovar et al. 2006, Nat Biotechnol) names proteases among the principal virulence factors but does not annotate the individual aprD/E/F genes, so it is cited as a system/strain reference (marked 'review') rather than for gene naming. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the secreted substrate AprA (= PSEEN1550, the effector), the AprI protease inhibitor (regulator/inhibitor, not transport apparatus), and the additional Apr-secreted substrate AprX. No maturation acyltransferase applies here (AprA is not an RTX-acylated toxin like HlyA, so there is no hlyC equivalent). Core three-component apparatus is fully documented gene-by-gene, hence COMPLETE; the only caveat is the cross-strain transfer from the canonical P. aeruginosa Apr system, standard under the separated-paper rule for this archetypal T1SS.
